questions.q: In massive MIMO, what is the optimal linear precoding strategy when perfect CSI is available at the transmitter?
questions.explanation: MMSE precoding maximizes SINR by balancing interference suppression and noise enhancement, optimal among linear precoders.

questions.q: What is the relationship between OFDM subcarrier spacing (Δf) and the maximum tolerable delay spread (τ_max)?
questions.explanation: Subcarrier spacing must be much less than the inverse of delay spread so each subcarrier experiences flat fading (coherence bandwidth >> Δf).

questions.q: In a Rayleigh fading channel, what is the distribution of the instantaneous SNR?
questions.explanation: If the channel amplitude is Rayleigh distributed, the power (and thus SNR) follows an exponential distribution.

questions.q: What is the primary advantage of LDPC codes over turbo codes at very high code rates?
questions.explanation: LDPC codes exhibit lower error floors at high code rates, making them preferred in 5G NR data channels.

questions.q: In compressed sensing for sparse channel estimation, what condition must the measurement matrix satisfy?
questions.explanation: RIP ensures that sparse signals can be recovered from underdetermined measurements with high probability.

questions.q: What is the capacity of a point-to-point MIMO channel with n_t transmit and n_r receive antennas at high SNR?
questions.explanation: At high SNR, MIMO capacity scales linearly with min(n_t, n_r), the multiplexing gain.

questions.q: In OFDM, what is the purpose of the cyclic prefix?
questions.explanation: The cyclic prefix absorbs multipath delay spread, converting a linear convolution into a circular one and eliminating ISI.

questions.q: What does the Wiener filter minimize in channel estimation?
questions.explanation: The Wiener filter (MMSE estimator) minimizes the mean squared error between the estimated and true channel.

questions.q: In a CDMA system, what limits capacity in the uplink?
choices: B) Near-far problem / multiple access interference
questions.explanation: Multiple access interference and the near-far problem are the dominant capacity-limiting factors in CDMA uplink.

questions.q: What is the Cramér-Rao Lower Bound (CRLB) used for in signal processing?
choices: B) Minimum achievable variance of an unbiased estimator
questions.explanation: CRLB provides a theoretical lower bound on the variance of any unbiased estimator of a parameter.

questions.q: In hybrid beamforming, why is a combination of analog and digital precoding used?
choices: B) To reduce the number of RF chains while maintaining spatial multiplexing
questions.explanation: Hybrid beamforming reduces hardware cost/power by using fewer RF chains than antennas, with analog phase shifters compensating.

questions.q: What is the key idea behind successive interference cancellation (SIC)?
choices: B) Decode strongest signal first, subtract it, then decode next
C) Use orthogonal codes for all users
questions.explanation: SIC decodes users sequentially from strongest to weakest, subtracting each decoded signal from the received composite.

questions.q: In deep learning-based CSI feedback, what is the role of the encoder at the UE?
choices: B) Compress CSI matrix into a low-dimensional codeword
questions.explanation: The encoder (typically a CNN or Transformer) compresses the high-dimensional CSI matrix into a compact representation for feedback.

questions.q: What is the diversity order of a 2x2 MIMO system using Alamouti space-time block coding?
questions.explanation: Alamouti STBC with 2 Tx and 2 Rx antennas achieves full diversity order of n_t × n_r = 4.

questions.q: In 5G NR, what is the purpose of the SSB (Synchronization Signal Block)?
choices: B) Initial cell search, timing/frequency synchronization, and beam management
questions.explanation: SSB carries PSS, SSS, and PBCH for initial access, synchronization, and beam sweeping in 5G NR.
